PollingStation.title: PollingStation
PollingStation.description: Represents a specific location in Kenya where citizens can register to vote or cast their ballot. This entity captures the geographical and descriptive details of such centers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PollingStation entity.

name.description: The official name of the polling or voter registration center.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude coordinate of the center.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude coordinate of the center.

address.description: The physical address or a descriptive location of the polling station, if available.

operatingHours.description: A description of the operating hours for voter registration or polling activities at this center.

type.description: Indicates the primary function of the center, e.g., 'registration', 'polling', or 'both'.

constituencyId.description: Reference to the Constituency entity to which this polling station belongs. (Relationship: Constituency 1:N PollingStation)

wardId.description: Reference to the Ward entity to which this polling station belongs. (Relationship: Ward 1:N PollingStation)

Constituency.title: Constituency
Constituency.description: Represents an administrative electoral subdivision in Kenya, often defined by a geographical polygon.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Constituency entity.

name.description: The official name of the constituency.

geoJsonBoundary.description: A GeoJSON string representing the polygonal boundary of the constituency, used for spatial queries and map display.

Ward.title: Ward
Ward.description: Represents a smaller administrative electoral subdivision within a Constituency in Kenya, defined by a geographical polygon.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ward entity.

name.description: The official name of the ward.

constituencyId.description: Reference to the Constituency entity to which this ward belongs. (Relationship: Constituency 1:N Ward)

geoJsonBoundary.description: A GeoJSON string representing the polygonal boundary of the ward, used for spatial queries and map display.

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Captures user-submitted feedback, comments, or ratings about a specific Polling Station, including issues and experiences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

pollingStationId.description: Reference to the PollingStation entity that this review is for. (Relationship: PollingStation 1:N Review)

userId.description: Identifier for the user who submitted the review, referencing an external authentication system. This field is optional and may be anonymous.

rating.description: An optional numerical rating given by the user, typically on a scale (e.g., 1-5).

comment.description: The textual feedback or comment provided by the user about their experience at the polling station.

timestamp.description: The date and time when the review was submitted.

issueFlags.description: An array of strings indicating specific issues or positive feedback reported by the user, e.g., 'long queues', 'helpful staff', 'closed'.

definition.description: Collection storing details for each voter registration and polling center. Includes denormalized 'constituencyId' and 'wardId' for efficient filtering, and 'geohash' for proximity queries. Publicly readable.

params.name: pollingStationId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific polling or registration station.

definition.description: Collection storing details and GeoJSON polygonal boundaries for each electoral constituency. Publicly readable.

params.name: constituencyId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific electoral constituency.

definition.description: Collection storing details and GeoJSON polygonal boundaries for each electoral ward. Includes 'constituencyId' for filtering. Publicly readable.

params.name: wardId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific electoral ward.

definition.description: Subcollection storing user-submitted reviews for a specific polling station. Includes denormalized 'pollingStationId' (implicit by path) and 'userId' for authorization independence. Publicly readable, but writeable only by the review's creator.

params.name: pollingStationId
params.description: Unique identifier of the parent polling station.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific review.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Mwanzo Vote Locator prioritizes public read access for core geographical data while securing user-generated content. Polling stations, constituencies, and wards are stored in top-level collections: `/pollingStations`, `/constituencies`, and `/wards`. This structural segregation ensures that all documents within these collections share the same security posture (public read-only), simplifying security rules and enabling efficient querying of location-based data.

To achieve **Authorization Independence**, particularly for user-generated `Reviews`, each `Review` document is placed in a subcollection (`/pollingStations/{pollingStationId}/reviews/{reviewId}`) and explicitly includes the `userId` of the creator. This denormalizes the ownership information directly into the document itself. Consequently, Firestore security rules can check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` directly for write operations without needing expensive or non-atomic `get()` calls to a parent `PollingStation` document or a separate user profile collection. This design ensures that creating, updating, or deleting a review can be an atomic operation, significantly improving reliability and debuggability.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters)** are supported across the design:
*   **Public Data (`/pollingStations`, `/constituencies`, `/wards`):** These collections are designed for unrestricted public read access. Security rules will simply allow all read operations. The application layer is responsible for client-side filtering (e.g., by distance, constituency, ward, or using geohash for proximity queries if implemented). This clearly separates access control from data filtering, making rules straightforward and efficient.
*   **Reviews (`/pollingStations/{pollingStationId}/reviews`):** All reviews within a station's subcollection are publicly readable. The path itself effectively filters reviews by `pollingStationId`. For write operations (create, update, delete), rules will enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches the `userId` stored within the `Review` document. This means users can only modify their own reviews, while anyone can read them. No complex rule-based filtering is required for listing reviews for a given station.
